title: Reverse Linked List
description:
## Reverse Linked List

Given the head of a singly linked list (as a plain integer list), reverse the list and return it as a plain integer list.

**Constraints:**
- The number of nodes is in the range `[0, 5000]`.
- `-5000 <= Node.val <= 5000`

solutions.label: Iterative — O(n)
solutions.explanation: Use two pointers: `prev` (starts as None) and `curr` (starts at head). In each step: save `curr.next`, point `curr.next` to `prev` (reversing the link), advance `prev` to `curr`, advance `curr` to the saved next. When `curr` is None, `prev` is the new head of the reversed list.

solutions.label: Recursive — O(n)
solutions.explanation: Recursively reverse the rest of the list. The last node becomes the new head. After the recursive call returns, `node.next` still points to the next node (now the tail of the reversed suffix). Set `node.next.next = node` to reverse that link, and `node.next = None` to avoid a cycle.

solutions.label: Iterative — O(n)
solutions.explanation: Use prev and curr pointers. In each step, save curr.next, reverse the link (curr.next = prev), then advance both pointers. When curr is null, prev is the new head.

hints: Use three pointers: prev (None initially), curr (head), and next_node (to save curr.next before overwriting it).
At each step, reverse the link: curr.next = prev, then advance both pointers.
